title: Raniji ki Baori
content: Raniji ki Baori is one of the most beautiful and intricately designed stepwells in Rajasthan. Built in 1699 by Queen Nathavati, it features multiple levels, ornate pillars, elegant arches, and a deep symmetrical descent. It is widely regarded as a masterpiece of Rajput architecture and one of the finest baoris in India.

title: 84 Pillared Cenotaph (Chaurasi Khamba)
content: The 84 Pillared Cenotaph, also known as Chaurasi Khamba ki Baori, is a unique royal memorial combined with a stepwell. Built in the 18th century, it features 84 symmetrical pillars supporting an open pavilion above a deep well. The geometric design and serene setting make it one of Bundi’s most striking architectural landmarks.

title: Garh Palace / Bundi Palace
content: Garh Palace is the grand royal palace of Bundi perched on a hillside overlooking the town. Famous for its exquisite wall murals, mirror work, jharokhas, and intricate frescoes depicting royal life and mythology, it offers breathtaking panoramic views of Bundi and the surrounding hills.
best_time: Morning or late afternoon for light and fewer crowds

title: Taragarh Fort
content: Taragarh Fort, also called Star Fort, is a massive 14th-century hill fort overlooking Bundi town. With thick defensive walls, watchtowers, and a grand gateway, it offers stunning panoramic views of the town, lakes, and surrounding hills — especially magical at sunrise or sunset.

title: Sukh Mahal
content: Sukh Mahal is an elegant white marble summer palace on the banks of Jait Sagar Lake. It is famously associated with Rudyard Kipling, who stayed here and drew inspiration for his novel 'Kim'. The palace features delicate jali windows, gardens, and serene lake views.
best_time: Evening for sunset over the lake

title: Kesar Bagh
content: Kesar Bagh is a royal garden complex containing beautifully carved cenotaphs (chhatris) of Bundi rulers and their families. The sandstone memorials are adorned with intricate carvings and set amidst peaceful gardens — a calm and reflective spot to appreciate Rajput royal history.

title: Jait Sagar Lake
content: Jait Sagar Lake is a scenic artificial lake near Sukh Mahal, surrounded by hills and gardens. It offers peaceful walking paths, boating opportunities, and beautiful sunset views — one of the most tranquil spots in Bundi for photography and relaxation.

title: Phool Sagar
content: Phool Sagar is a royal summer palace complex with beautiful gardens, fountains, and lake views. It features elegant architecture, lush greenery, and serene surroundings — a lesser-visited but highly picturesque spot in Bundi.

title: Dhabhai Kund
content: Dhabhai Kund is a large, traditional stepwell located in the heart of Bundi town. Less touristy than Raniji ki Baori, it is an authentic and functional water structure with simple yet elegant architecture — a great example of local water conservation heritage.

title: Naval Sagar Lake
content: Naval Sagar is a serene artificial lake in Bundi with island-like views and surrounding hills. It is a quiet spot ideal for calm evening walks, photography, and enjoying the peaceful atmosphere away from the main tourist areas.
